content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

August 3, 1999


Steven R. Sanders
840 Neil Drive
Hollister, California 95023

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-99-197

Dear Mr. Sanders:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the Section 84308
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

QUESTION

Does Section 84308, which triggers disqualification in certain circumstances due to the
receipt of campaign contributions, apply to you in your current position as San Benito County
Counsel?

CONCLUSION

Section 84308 does not apply to you as an appointed county counsel, as discussed below.

FACTS

You are the San Benito County Counsel and are responsible for providing legal services
to San Benito County departments. Your representation extends from the San Benito County
Board of Supervisors to all San Benito County elected officers and appointed department heads.
You do not sit on any committees, boards or commissions as a voting member. You do not make
any governmental decisions or approve permits. However, you advise the decision-makers
regarding the legal effect of a decision.


1 Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. A-99-197
Page 2

You were appointed to the position of county counsel by the board of supervisors and are
an employee of the county. You report directly to the board of supervisors, which is the
appointing authority under the county's personnel ordinance. There are three deputy county
counsels.

You intend to be a candidate for Superior Court Judge in San Benito County in the
upcoming March 2000 election.

ANALYSIS

Section 84308 disqualifies any "officer" of a public agency, who is running for elective
office, from participating in certain "entitlement" decisions affecting his or her campaign
contributors. Section 84308(b) provides that:

"No officer of an agency shall accept, solicit, or direct a
contribution of more than two hundred fifty dollars ($250) from
any party, or his or her agent, or from any participant, or his or her
agent, while a proceeding involving a license, permit, or other
entitlement for use is pending before the agency and for three
months following the date a final decision is rendered in the
proceeding if the officer knows or has reason to know that the
participant has a financial interest, as that term is used in Article 1
(commencing with Section 87100) of Chapter 7. This prohibition
shall apply regardless of whether the officer accepts, solicits, or
directs the contribution for himself or herself, or on behalf of any
other officer, or on behalf of any candidate for office or on behalf
of any committee."

The statute itself defines many of its terms. "Officer" is defined as:

"[A]ny elected or appointed officer of an agency, any alternate to
an elected or appointed officer of an agency, and any candidate for
elective office in an agency." (Section 84308(a)(4).)

Section 84308(a)(3) defines "agency" as:

"[A]n agency as defined in Section 82003 except that it does not
include the courts or any agency in the judicial branch of
government, local governmental agencies whose members are
directly elected by the voters, the Legislature, the Board of
Equalization, or constitutional officers. However, this section

File No. A-99-197
Page 3

applies to any person who is a member of an exempted agency but
is acting as a voting member of another agency." (Emphasis
added.)

Section 82003 defines "agency" as any state or local government agency. "Local
government agency" is defined in Section 82041 as:

"[A] county, city or district of any kind including school district,
or any other local or regional political subdivision, or any
department, division, bureau, office, board, commission or other
agency of the foregoing."

However, Section 84308(a)(3) expressly exempts local governmental agencies whose
members are directly elected by the voters. This includes the elected members of the county
board of supervisors.

The issue is whether a county counsel is an officer of an agency for purposes of Section
84308. Pursuant to Regulation 18438.1(d), an officer of an agency includes only those persons
who:

"(1) Serve as members of governmental boards and
commissions; or
(2) Serve as the head of an agency. A member of the Governor's
Cabinet is not an officer under Government Code Section 84308
when he or she is acting in the capacity of agency secretary;
however, the person is an officer when he or she acts as a
department director or as a member of any board or commission."

Government Code Section 24000 enumerates the elected officers of a county. This
includes members of the board of supervisors. (Government Code Section 24000(o).) The board
of supervisors then has the authority to appoint a county counsel (Government Code Section
27640) and assistants to enable him to perform his duties. (Government Code Section 27644.)

You report directly to the board of supervisors and render legal advice to the board. In
your role as legal advisor to the county, you also advise officers and employees of special
districts organized within the county. (Government Code Section 27645.) In your capacity as
county counsel, you serve the board of supervisors whose elected members are specifically
exempt under Section 84308(a)(3). Since you serve the board and other county officers and
employees in a legal advisory capacity, you are not the head of an agency within the meaning of
Regulation 18438.1(d). Therefore, you are not an officer of an agency for purposes of Section
84308.

File No. A-99-197
Page 4

In addition, for Section 84308 to apply, there must be a "proceeding involving a license, permit or other entitlement for use" pending before an agency. (Section 84308(b) and (c); Regulation 18438.2(a).) A proceeding is "pending before an agency" when an issue is submitted to the jurisdiction of an agency for its determination or action and when the officers of the agency are either required by law to make a decision or the matter is submitted to the officers for their decision. (Regulation 18438.2(b).)

You stated that as the county counsel, you do not sit on any boards or commissions and you do not participate in any governmental decisions. You render legal advice to county officers. The county counsel's office does not have proceedings involving a license, permit or other entitlement for use pending before it and you do not participate in any determinations or decisions involving such proceedings. Therefore, Section 84308 does not apply to you.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

[signature: Jill Stecher/jts]

By: Jill Stecher
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

SGC:JS:tls
